regime: Divergent Rotation / NQ Flips Short, ES Makes New Highs
summary: Cool inflation, and the Nasdaq sold it anyway. June PPI came in soft across the board (5.5% YoY against 6.2% expected, MoM -0.3%, core 4.7%), overnight pushed MNQ through 30,000 to a 30,062.5 high, and then the RTH session took the whole thing back and closed -0.29% at 29,708. The trend stop flipped short on the very first RTH hour. What makes today worth studying is what the S&P did instead: MES closed +0.33% at 7,615.75, made a 7,626 high, and never came close to losing its stop. That split is the read. Money left megacap tech and stayed in the market rather than leaving it.

event.name: Producer Price Index (June 2026)
event.surprise: COOL across the board, on headline, MoM and core
event.nqReaction: Inverted. MNQ had already cleared 30,000 overnight to a 30,062.5 high on the CPI carry-through. The 12:00 UTC PPI bar closed 29,980.25 and held, then the 14:00 UTC RTH bar closed 29,631.25, flipping the trend stop short. MNQ finished -0.29% at 29,708 after a 495.50-point RTH range and a 29,396.75 low.
event.crossAsset: The tell is that this was not an inflation repricing. MES made new highs (+0.33%, 7,626 high), MCL rose 0.46% even though the PPI energy component fell 6.4%, and MGC added only 0.21%. If the tape were trading the data, crude and gold would have led. They did not. The selling was tech-specific, which reads as rotation rather than macro.

observe.name: ES/NQ confirmation
observe.note: Yesterday both flipped long together and that was called real confirmation. Today it split, MES long and MNQ short. The confirmation that mattered was one session deep.

observe.name: GAP_NQ (gap context, outside the NAP stack)
observe.note: The weekend risk-off gap toward Friday's 30,069 was fully closed overnight at 30,062.5, and price was rejected there immediately. A gap fill that arrives and reverses at the level is the setup doing its job.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ
observe.note: Outside the Net Alpha Pro stack, logged for context only.

risk.throttleState: REDUCED, and the reason changed. Yesterday the book was throttled because the flip was one session old. Today that exact risk paid out, on the instrument that led the flip. The stack is split, so a single directional bet across the four is no longer supported by the data.
risk.ddProximity: Not binding. The book entered flat and the full daily budget is intact.
risk.dailyLossBudget: Intact. The 495.50-point MNQ range means position size should come down, not up, to hold the same dollar risk.
risk.sizing: Take each instrument on its own stop, not as one basket. MCL is the only clean trend (stop unchanged at 78.59, never breached on an RTH close). MES is intact but is now the sole index long. MGC already whipsawed today and deserves the smallest size of the four. MNQ's short flip is hours old, and a fresh flip is the least reliable state a trend stop has.
risk.openRisk: Flat into the close. No overnight exposure carried.
risk.note: Week ahead: Retail Sales lands Thursday 8:30 ET, forecast +0.2% against +0.9% prior, with jobless claims and Philly Fed alongside. Two cool inflation prints in two days produced two opposite tape reactions, which is a signal that this market is not trading the data cleanly. Avoid new risk in the 8:30 window and let the reaction settle before sizing up.

psychology: Yesterday's map said 'above 29,536 long' and the market paid that off overnight, right up through 30,000. Then it took every point back. This is where a good plan gets you hurt if you stop reading it. The map also carried a stop, and the stop is the part that mattered today. Traders who anchored on being right about the level gave back a 500-point round trip defending it. The flip is one session old in both directions now, which is exactly what a two-way tape does to fresh trends. Being early and being wrong feel identical while you're in it. Let the stop tell you which one it was.

MCL1!80.4079.810.59DOWN13:5579.80Yes

MNQ's OR is the one to study. It broke down at 13:45, snapped straight back through the range to the 29,892.25 session high, and then failed and trended down for the rest of the day. A break, a reclaim, and then a real move the other way. The reclaim is what trapped people.

fullReport.title: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter)
fullReport.html: The regime filter is the whole story here. The 15min momentum-long setup had a green backdrop into the 12:00 UTC PPI bar with MNQ printing 29,980.25 and holding above a 29,793.24 ratcheted stop. Two hours later the stop had flipped short. A filter that reads risk-on from the overnight tape and does not re-check after the RTH open is a filter that buys the high. No qualifying long breakout survived the session on MNQ.

fullReport.title: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ)
fullReport.html:
LevelValueNote
AVWAP29,633.14Session anchor
Sigma67.95Sample std of RTH typical prices
+2s / +3s29,769.05 / 29,837.01The 29,892.25 high tagged +3.81s
-2s / -3s29,497.23 / 29,429.28The 29,396.75 low tagged -3.48s
Close29,708.00+1.10s

MNQ traded from +3.81 sigma to -3.48 sigma and closed at +1.10 sigma inside one RTH session. That is a full-range reversion and it is the widest sigma traverse in weeks. The honest caveat: this band is anchored on the closed session, so both tags are visible only in hindsight. Volume is unavailable on the continuous contract, so the AVWAP is an unweighted typical-price mean and the bands are price-based.

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked
fullReport.html:
#Row (as written Jul 14)StatusGradeWhat happened
1MNQ holds above the 29,536 stop and the 29,798 AVWAP, then extends toward the 29,896 RTH high and the 30,000 roundTRIGGEREDMIXEDIt held and it delivered, then it gave it all back. Globex ran to 30,062.5, clearing both targets. The RTH session reversed the whole move to a 29,396.75 low. The stated void (a 2-close back under 29,536) never fired; only one hourly close printed below it, at 29,513.
2A hot PPI drives a 2-close back below the 29,798 AVWAP and the 29,567 RTH low, and the pullback resumes toward 29,536 and the 29,303 shelfNOT TRIGGEREDn/aThe PPI was cool, not hot, so the condition never armed. Worth logging anyway: the downside happened regardless. MNQ lost the AVWAP and the 29,567 low and traded to 29,396.75. The direction was right and the reason was wrong.
3MES reclaims and holds above the 7,602 record with the 7,588 AVWAP as support, and continuation opens fresh highs above 7,604TRIGGEREDMIXEDThe fresh highs came (7,626) and the close held at 7,615.75. The 'holds' part did not stay clean. Two hourly closes slipped under 7,602 midday and the 7,571.75 low cut through the 7,588 AVWAP before the reclaim.
4MGC holds the 4,044 stop but stays capped under the 4,070 AVWAP, and chops between 4,044 and the 4,100 psychTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUTThe cap held exactly as mapped. Gold chopped 4,032.8 to 4,089 and closed 4,067, still under the 4,070 AVWAP. It briefly lost 4,044 (one hourly close at 4,040.1) without confirming, and never reached 4,100.
5MCL holds above the 78.59 stop, and the bid can retest the 80.28 high and the 81.28 overnight highTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUTThe cleanest row of the day. Every RTH hourly close stayed above 78.59 (the 78.18 low pierced it intraday but no close confirmed), and crude ran to 80.70, clearing the 80.28 target. The 81.28 second target went untouched.

Score: 5 rows, 4 triggered, 2 played out, 2 mixed, 0 failed. MTD tally: 33 triggered, 18 played out, 4 failed.

Filters Thursday: July 16 is a Retail Sales (June) day, printing 8:30 ET alongside jobless claims and Philly Fed, before the cash open. HOF, MID, BNC and BIR are calendar-eligible. Per HTA prop rules, avoid new risk in the 8:30 window and treat the open as event-contaminated until the initial reaction settles. HOF is SHORT ONLY and now has a trend-aligned backdrop on MNQ, so it needs a genuine downside OR break to qualify. The HOF dead zone 12:00 to 14:00 ET applies and MID runs only in the 9:30 to 10:30 ET window.

Calendar: Retail Sales (June) Thursday July 16 at 8:30 ET, forecast +0.2% against +0.9% prior, with initial and continuing jobless claims and the Philly Fed index alongside.

yesterdaysMapScore.note: Graded the Jul 14 map (targeting Jul 15). Rows 4 and 5 TRIGGERED / PLAYED OUT (MGC stayed capped under its 4,070 AVWAP and chopped as mapped; MCL held 78.59 on every RTH close and cleared the 80.28 target at 80.70). Rows 1 and 3 TRIGGERED / MIXED: MNQ hit both upside targets overnight at 30,062.5 then reversed the entire move and flipped short, though the stated 2-close void under 29,536 never fired; MES made the mapped fresh highs at 7,626 but broke the 'holds above 7,602' and 7,588 AVWAP clauses intraday before reclaiming. Row 2 NOT TRIGGERED: it required a hot PPI and the print was cool, though the mapped downside occurred anyway for a different reason.
